title: Ajora ASPEC - Automation Specification
description: Platform-neutral automation specification built on top of the Ajora action catalogue.

aspec_version.description: Semantic version of the ASPEC spec used for this automation.

credentials.description: Authentication credentials configured for this automation.

resources.description: External resources selected by the user (accounts, spreadsheets, folders, etc.).

trigger.description: The single trigger step that starts the automation.

steps.description: Ordered list of non-trigger steps in the automation.

metadata.description: High-level metadata about this automation specification.

description.description: Short description of what this automation does.

created_by.description: Identifier of the creator (user id, email, etc.).

updated_at.description: Last update timestamp in ISO 8601 format.

tags.description: Optional tags for grouping or searching automations.

credential_id.description: Reference to the credential in the user's vault.

status.description: Status of the credential (ok, missing, invalid).

resource.description: A selected external resource (e.g., Gmail account, specific spreadsheet, folder).

type.description: Resource type within the service (e.g., account, spreadsheet, folder).

id.description: Service-specific ID of this resource (e.g., spreadsheetId).

step.description: A single step in the automation, referring to a toolbox adapter. Assumed to be fully configured.

step_id.description: Unique identifier of this step within the automation.

adapter_id.description: ID of the adapter from the toolbox (e.g., email.trigger.gmail).

adapter_name.description: Optional human-readable name of the adapter (redundant with toolbox).

service.description: Service used by this adapter (e.g., gmail, google_sheets).

description.description: Optional description of the role of this step in this specific automation.

is_trigger.description: Indicates whether this step is the automation's trigger.

trigger_mode.description: Optional trigger mode when is_trigger = true (e.g., polling, webhook, event).

input_from.description: Reference to the step (and optional alias) providing the input for this step.

oneOf.description: Multiple input references for future use.

outputs.description: Logical output types this step produces (e.g., email_object, append_result).

credential_ref.description: Reference to the credential key in the credentials object that this step should use.

parameter_origin.description: Indicates where each parameter value came from (user, default, inferred, system).

annotations.description: Optional free-form annotations or hints for UI, debugging, etc.

configuredParameters.description: Parameter values configured for a specific step.

connection.description: Represents a logical connection between two steps.

type.description: Optional connection type (e.g. main, error, branch).
